title: The second altermagnet candidate in organic conductors: $κ$-(BEDT-TTF)$_2$$m$-HOOCC$_6$H$_4$SO$_3$
authors: Kohei Tokura, Takato Masuta, Kazushi Aoyama, Hiroki Akutsu, Yasuhiro Nakazawa, Scott S. Turner
abstract: We have developed a novel BEDT-TTF-based organic conductor, $κ$-(BEDT-TTF)$_2 m$-HOOCC$_6$H$_4$SO$_3$ ($κ$-$m$-SBA), and propose it as a candidate for altermagnet. Tight-binding band calculations of $κ$-$m$-SBA provide a $t'/t$ of 1.01 at 100 K, indicating that the spin structure is closely aligned to an equilateral triangle ($t'/t= 1$). While most $κ$-type BEDT-TTF-based salts become spin liquids due to the spin frustration caused by the triangular lattice, $κ$-$m$-SBA surprisingly shows a weak ferromagnetic transition at $T_\mathrm{N} = 14$ K due to a canted antiferromagnetic (AFM) spin structure. Until recently, $κ$-(BEDT-TTF)$_2$Cu[N(CN)$_2$]Cl ($κ$-Cl) was the only $κ$-type organic conductor known to exhibit this order, and it is also recognized as the first candidate for altermagnetism in organic conductors. This was theoretically predicted by Naka et al. in 2019, who demonstrated that $κ$-type organic conductors can be candidates for altermagnetism if they display such order. Consequently, $κ$-$m$-SBA can be considered the second candidate for altermagnetism in organic conductors. Furthermore, numerical calculations demonstrate a characteristic of altermagnets in $κ$-$m$-SBA, namely spin splitting of energy bands.
summary: The researchers synthesized a new organic conductor, kappa-m-SBA, which serves as the second known candidate for altermagnetism in organic salts. By combining experimental measurements with numerical simulations, they demonstrated that the material exhibits the spin-dependent band splitting characteristic of altermagnets. This finding expands the known library of organic materials with unique spin-orbit-like electronic structures without requiring heavy elements.
main_problem: The identification of a new candidate for altermagnetism in organic conductors and explaining why the material kappa-m-SBA exhibits a canted antiferromagnetic transition instead of a spin liquid state.
main_result: The discovery of kappa-m-SBA as the second organic altermagnet candidate, demonstrating characteristic spin-dependent band splitting due to the interplay of AFM order and anisotropic electron hopping.
method: The study combines electrocrystallization synthesis, SQUID magnetometry, and electrical resistivity measurements with first-principles (OpenMX) and tight-binding band calculations.
model_or_system: A novel organic conductor, kappa-(BEDT-TTF)2m-HOOCC6H4SO3 (kappa-m-SBA), modeled using a Hubbard model on a dimerized, nearly equilateral triangular lattice.
key_observables: Magnetic susceptibility (chi-T), magnetization (M-H), electrical resistivity, spin-dependent band splitting, and the frustration parameter (t'/t).
important_parameters: Neel temperature (TN) = 14 K, transfer integrals (t, t', tb, tp, tq), Coulomb interaction (U = 0.54 eV), and Mott gap (0.22 eV).
main_assumptions: The slightly canted magnetic structure is approximated as a collinear antiferromagnetic order to evaluate symmetry-driven altermagnetism.
figures_summary: Figure 5 shows energy band dispersion with spin splitting; Figure S8 shows the anionic layer crystal structure and dipole orientation; Figure S9 shows the classification of polar organic conductors.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the new material, details its synthesis and structural properties, analyzes its magnetic and electronic transport properties, and concludes with numerical calculations demonstrating altermagnetic signatures.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
